name: civic_technology_predictive_queue_begins_cedar_rig_utl
premise: A Civic Technology response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving civic technology predictive queue begins cedar rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Mei Chen
agents.identity: I am Mei Chen, accountable for a defensible call in Civic Technology when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Priya Santos
agents.identity: I am Priya Santos, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Civic Technology.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Mei Chen must decide under rights vs utility. What does Mei Chen do?
